content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5602        322-5660           322-5901           322-6441              322-6444


                                            February 25, 1981


Honorable Erich Auerbach
Judge of the Municipal Court
110 North Grand
Los Angeles, CA 90012
                                                          A-81-03-009

Dear Judge Auerbach:

     This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation
of February 2, 1981.

     According to my notes, you asked if the following items
are reportable on your Statement of Economic Interest:

     Treasury bills
     Mutual funds and bonds
     Federal and military pensions

     The Political Reform Act does not require interests in
a diversified mutual fund registered with the Securities and
Exchange Commission, or any bond or other debt instrument
issued by any government or government agency to be reported
(see Government Code Section 82034).

     Also, the Commission staff has determined that Treasury
bills would be exempt from disclosure since they would be
considered a debt instrument issued by the government.

     Also, income from the Army pension plan would not be
reportable, since payments received under a defined benefit
pension plan qualified under the Internal Revenue Section
401(a) are exempt from disclosure (see Government Code Section
82030).

                                            Yours truly,

                                            [signed]

                                            Alice Hughes
                                            Documents Examiner

sections.question: Are the following items reportable on your Statement of Economic Interest: Treasury bills, Mutual funds and bonds, Federal and military pensions?
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not require interests in a diversified mutual fund registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission, or any bond or other debt instrument issued by any government or government agency to be reported (see Government Code Section 82034). Treasury bills would be exempt from disclosure since they would be considered a debt instrument issued by the government. Income from the Army pension plan would not be reportable, since payments received under a defined benefit pension plan qualified under the Internal Revenue Section 401(a) are exempt from disclosure (see Government Code Section 82030).
sections.question_synthetic: The judge asked whether treasury bills, mutual funds, bonds, and federal/military pension income must be reported on the Statement of Economic Interest form.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that mutual funds, bonds, treasury bills, and federal/military pension income are all exempt from disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act. These items are either considered government-issued debt instruments or qualify for pension plan exemptions under applicable law.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document has two versions (one partially garbled/scanned and one cleaner version). Extracted from cleaner version. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but structure is clear. Minor OCR artifacts in first version do not affect extraction from second version.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are the following items reportable on your Statement of Economic Interest: Treasury bills, Mutual funds and bonds, Federal and military pensions?

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not require interests in a diversified mutual fund registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission, or any bond or other debt instrument issued by any government or government agency to be reported (see Government Code Section 82034). Treasury bills would be exempt from disclosure since they would be considered a debt instrument issued by the government. Income from the Army pension plan would not be reportable, since payments received under a defined benefit pension plan qualified under the Internal Revenue Section 401(a) are exempt from disclosure (see Government Code Section 82030).

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC confirmation letter to Judge Erich Auerbach regarding reportable items on his Statement of Economic Interest, clarifying that treasury bills, mutual funds, bonds, and pension income are exempt from disclosure under the Political Reform Act.
